diagrams.title: Vector Space R² with Vector Operations
diagrams.description: A 2D coordinate system showing vectors as arrows from the origin, demonstrating vector addition using the parallelogram rule and scalar multiplication. This visualizes the fundamental operations that define a vector space.
elements: resultant vector a+b (dashed arrow forming parallelogram)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the closure properties of a vector space: vector addition and scalar multiplication produce vectors that remain in the space

diagrams.title: Subspaces of R²
diagrams.description: Three distinct cases in R²: (1) the entire plane R² as a subspace, (2) a line through the origin as a 1D subspace, (3) the origin alone as the zero subspace. Also shows a line NOT through the origin to illustrate why it fails to be a subspace.
elements: line y=2x+1 NOT through origin (invalid subspace)
label: 'Not a Subspace' for invalid case
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the subspace criteria: must contain zero vector, be closed under addition and scalar multiplication

diagrams.title: Subspaces in R³ - Planes and Lines Through Origin
diagrams.description: A 3D coordinate system showing various subspaces: the XY, XZ, and YZ planes as 2D subspaces, lines through the origin as 1D subspaces, and the origin point as the 0D subspace. This extends understanding to three dimensions.
diagrams.purpose: Shows that subspaces can have different dimensions and visualizes how higher-dimensional subspaces contain lower-dimensional ones, all passing through the origin
